claims.claim_text: Module-level doc comments (## lines) placed at the top of the file, before imports and declarations, are recognized by nim doc as module documentation.
claims.evaluation_notes: nim doc produces HTML with class='module-desc' containing the text.

claims.claim_text: Block doc comment syntax ##[ ... ]## works for module-level documentation.
Proc doc comments (## lines) placed immediately after the signature line and before the implementation body are attached to that proc by nim doc.
claims.evaluation_notes: Doc text appears in HTML attached to the proc.

claims.claim_text: runnableExamples: blocks placed after doc text and before implementation statements compile and execute correctly during nim doc.
claims.evaluation_notes: nim doc succeeds with runnableExamples inside proc body.

claims.claim_text: Inside type blocks, an inline trailing ## comment on a declaration line attaches to that symbol (e.g., Hash* = int ## A hash value.).
claims.evaluation_notes: Doc text found in HTML output.

claims.claim_text: Multi-line ## continuation lines indented under a declaration attach to the same symbol.
claims.evaluation_notes: Both lines of doc text appear in HTML.

claims.claim_text: nim doc path/to/module.nim renders HTML documentation from ## comments in the source.
claims.evaluation_notes: HTML files generated successfully for all test modules.

claims.claim_text: Exported symbols (marked with *) appear in nim doc output; non-exported symbols do not.
claims.evaluation_notes: Exported proc doc found; non-exported proc doc absent from HTML.

claims.claim_text: runnableExamples code is compiled and executed during nim doc, and a failing doAssert inside runnableExamples causes nim doc to fail.
claims.evaluation_notes: nim doc exited non-zero when runnableExamples contained doAssert false.

claims.claim_text: runnableExamples("-d:flag") passes the given compile flag during example compilation.
claims.evaluation_notes: nim doc succeeded with -d:myflag; example used defined(myflag) successfully.

claims.claim_text: Enum value doc comments on the same line as the value attach to that specific enum value.
claims.evaluation_notes: Enum value doc text found in HTML.

claims.claim_text: Object field doc comments placed after the field declaration attach to that field in rendered docs.
claims.evaluation_notes: Field doc text found in HTML.

claims.claim_text: Const doc comments on the same line as the const declaration attach to that const symbol.
claims.evaluation_notes: Const doc text found in HTML.

claims.claim_text: Top-level runnableExamples: placed after module docs compiles and runs correctly.
claims.evaluation_notes: nim doc succeeded with top-level runnableExamples block.

claims.claim_text: A ## doc comment placed immediately above a proc signature is treated as module documentation rather than attaching to that proc.
claims.evaluation_notes: Rendered as module-desc in HTML for the test module instead of proc-attached documentation.

claims.claim_text: Inside a type block, a standalone ## line above a type declaration does not attach to that type in rendered docs.
claims.evaluation_notes: The exported type appears in rendered docs, but the above-declaration text is absent.

claims.claim_text: Inside a const block, a standalone ## line above a const declaration does not attach to that const in rendered docs.
claims.evaluation_notes: The exported const appears in rendered docs, but the above-declaration text is absent.

claims.claim_text: A module-style ## comment placed after imports can still render as module documentation, so rendered HTML alone does not prove correct top-of-file placement.
claims.evaluation_notes: The after-import comment still rendered as module-desc, which means source placement must be checked separately when standard layout matters.
